title: MP respawn clears raw player+0x1064 instead of gunammooff (raw-cast sweep)
repro: Multiplayer respawn: gunammooff is cleared via raw player+0x1064 (native mis-offset) instead of the named field, so the gun-render suppression flag may not reset.

history.note: ASM-audit batch-6 raw-cast sweep, same class as FID-0085/0087/0088/0091. Retail US ASM mp_respawn_handler (7F08897C: sw $zero, 0x1064($t6=g_CurrentPlayer)) clears g_CurrentPlayer+0x1064. N64 player+0x1064 = gunammooff (after last_z_trigger_timer@0x105C, copiedgoldeneye@0x1060). On the 64-bit port layout the uniform +0x150 post-hands shift means the raw write misses gunammooff and corrupts an unrelated field -- while the gun-render gate (gun.c:32121 'if (g_CurrentPlayer->gunammooff != 0) return gdl;'), gun.c:31114/31118, and initBondDATA.c:322 use the named field, so a respawn could leave the first-person gun un-rendered. Fix: write g_CurrentPlayer->gunammooff = 0. Default ON; GE007_NO_GUNAMMOOFF_RESPAWN_FIX restores the raw write byte-identically. Struct-layout lock: offsetof(struct player,gunammooff) != 0x1064.
ASM-derived offset locked; see finding note.
One-line guard/field fix behind default-ON flag with byte-identical negative control (see finding note).

Task-specified validation bar GREEN: test_struct_layout PASS (field locks hold), sim_state_hash PASS, sim_invariance_gate PASS (dam1 f600 OFF==ON c83e78eabba5be2e), tape_regression PASS 7/7 byte-exact (zero sim-hash shift -- affected weapon/path not in any committed tape), ROM-free ctest green (only pre-existing out-of-tree intro_parse_digest CWD artifact). Negative control restores legacy raw offset byte-identically.
